expectations.text: Code imports addUpdateLead from scripts/lib/influencersoft.mjs (the project's existing client lib, not raw fetch)
expectations.evidence: Option 1 and 2 both import addUpdateLead from './lib/influencersoft.mjs' — the project's existing helper, not a raw fetch implementation.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes exactly 'customer:etsy' (the trigger tag for post-purchase-etsy-buyer sequence — case-sensitive, colon-separated)
expectations.evidence: add_tags: 'customer:etsy,source:etsy,...' appears in both Option 1 and Option 2. Exact string, correct case.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes a product tag in the format 'product:tax-001-mileage-log' (lowercase, hyphenated SKU slug — not 'TAX-001' or 'product:TAX-001')
expectations.evidence: product:tax-001-mileage-log present in skuMap and tags array. Correct format: lowercase, hyphenated slug.

expectations.text: Answer mentions or references tag-dictionary.md as the source of truth for tag strings
expectations.evidence: JSDoc comment '// Map SKU to product tag per tag-dictionary.md §3' and Tag Reference section cites 'infrastructure/influencersoft/tag-dictionary.md (121 lines, authoritative)'.

expectations.text: Answer references the tenant 'kebron' or the URL kebron.influencersoft.com somewhere (so the reader knows which tenant the call hits)
expectations.evidence: n8n section shows POST URL 'https://kebron.influencersoft.com/api/AddUpdateLead' and API Key section references IS_TENANT=kebron.

expectations.text: Answer does NOT log the full POST body or rpsKey (security: key leakage risk; the lib enforces this but the answer should respect it too)
expectations.evidence: Console.log calls only emit email and success message. No POST body, no rpsKey logged anywhere.

expectations.text: Mentions corporate-domain sender requirement (free domains like Gmail/Yahoo get DMARC-rejected)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 header: 'Sender Domain Is Corporate, Not Gmail/Yahoo — DMARC authentication rejects free-domain senders across most email providers.' Explicit warning with fix instructions.

expectations.text: Mentions sender confirmation — that new sender emails are dead until the IS confirmation link is clicked
expectations.evidence: Section 1: 'IS sent a confirmation link to that mailbox... Without this, sequences silently fail to send — recipients get nothing (not spam, just dead).' Named as #1 check.

expectations.text: Mentions DKIM, SPF, AND DMARC DNS records (all three; not just 'DNS records')
expectations.evidence: Section 3 names all three explicitly: 'SPF record exists', 'DKIM record exists', 'DMARC record exists' — each with format examples and separate bullets.

expectations.text: Mentions FBL (Feedback Loop) setup
expectations.evidence: Section 4 is titled 'Feedback Loop (FBL) Is Enabled' and covers setup, path (Campaigns → Settings → FBL), and consequences of missing it.

expectations.text: Includes the FBL gotcha: the FBL mailbox MUST be brand-new (IS auto-deletes incoming mail). This is the most consequential deliverability mistake.
expectations.evidence: 'Do NOT reuse an existing mailbox or IS will auto-delete all its contents. Must be brand new.' — exact gotcha stated under section 4 'If misconfigured (CRITICAL warning in gotcha #4)'.

expectations.text: Mentions Tech Tuesday or support@influencersoft.com escalation path
expectations.evidence: Key Resources section: 'Tech Tuesday — Live support call with IS team; can screen-share to configure DNS live.' Tech Tuesday named explicitly.

expectations.text: Points the user to a specific menu path (e.g., Campaigns → Settings, or Campaigns → Analytics) rather than only generic advice
expectations.evidence: Multiple specific paths given: 'Campaigns → Settings → Senders', 'Campaigns → Settings → FBL', 'Campaigns → Analytics → Broadcasts Message Analytics', 'Campaigns → Settings → Auto-clean'.

expectations.text: Warns about prefix-slug collision: IS rejects names sharing a prefix with an existing field
expectations.evidence: 'InfluencerSoft rejects custom field names that share a prefix with any existing field. Your name referral_source is safe IF no other field starts with referral_.' — explicit warning with the exact mechanism.

expectations.text: Mentions the stale UI error: the form holds the error from the previous attempt and reports the LAST collision, so refresh the page between adds
expectations.evidence: 'the form will hold that error even after you fix the name and try again. The error message may not reflect your current attempt — it shows the LAST collision instead.' Fix: 'Always refresh the Custom Fields page between each field addition attempt.'

expectations.text: References the existing custom-field naming convention: short distinct stems (sku_code, sku_label, bought_on, order_ref, xsell_name, xsell_url, pack_name)
expectations.evidence: 'The project standardizes on short, distinct stems: sku_, bought_, order_, xsell_, pack_.' Also lists all 7 existing fields by name: sku_code, sku_label, bought_on, order_ref, xsell_name, xsell_url, pack_name.

expectations.text: Gives the exact menu path: Contacts → Custom Fields
expectations.evidence: 'Go to: Contacts → Custom Fields (in IS UI)' — exact path given under section 3 Menu Path.

expectations.text: Recommends a shorter or differently-prefixed name (e.g., ref_src, source_ref) to reduce collision risk with future fields
expectations.evidence: 'Consider: ref_src (matches the short-stem style) / source_ref (alternative prefix). The longer name is valid, but shorter stems reduce future collision risk if you add referral_status, referral_date, etc. later.'

expectations.text: Identifies the correct API endpoint: API 1.0 AddGood (not API 2.0 — products require legacy API)
expectations.evidence: TL;DR states 'InfluencerSoft's AddGood endpoint (API 1.0)'. Capability matrix table also lists AddGood under API 1.0.

expectations.text: Mentions API 1.0 hash auth (MD5 of buildQuery + username + apikey concatenation)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 header says 'Build the HMAC Wrapper' and code uses crypto.createHmac('sha256', apiSecret). Algorithm is HMAC-SHA256, not MD5. The skill (api-quickref.md §3) explicitly states MD5.

expectations.text: Mentions the existing project script: infrastructure/influencersoft/push_products.js
expectations.evidence: Resources section references 'ops/influencersoft-api-probe.md' and 'modules.md' but never mentions infrastructure/influencersoft/push_products.js.

expectations.text: WARNS the user that AddGood may be disabled per-account (returns error_code 2) and that as of 2026-05-08 it was disabled for the kebron tenant
expectations.evidence: Gotchas section lists 5 items (legacy API, hash sort order, no sandbox, rate limiting, field names). No mention of error_code 2 or per-account disablement. The critical 'endpoint disabled for kebron' warning is absent.

expectations.text: Provides the escalation path to enable AddGood: email support@influencersoft.com or raise at Tech Tuesday
expectations.evidence: No mention of support@influencersoft.com or Tech Tuesday anywhere in the answer. The 'Contact InfluencerSoft support' suggestion appears only in the unrelated n8n section context.

expectations.text: Suggests testing with a small batch first (or dry-run mode) before pushing all 70 products
expectations.evidence: Testing Checklist step: 'POST canary product to AddGood (production, no rollback)' and Step 2 says 'Test with 1 canary product first — no sandbox, production-only'.

expectations.text: Code imports addUpdateLead from scripts/lib/influencersoft.mjs (the project's existing client lib, not raw fetch)
expectations.evidence: Answer builds its own fetch wrapper from scratch using URLSearchParams and raw fetch. Never imports from scripts/lib/influencersoft.mjs or references the project client lib.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes exactly 'customer:etsy' (the trigger tag for post-purchase-etsy-buyer sequence — case-sensitive, colon-separated)
expectations.evidence: Tags used are 'etsy-buyer', 'sku-tax-001', 'post-purchase-sequence', 'mileage-log-buyer'. The required 'customer:etsy' trigger tag is absent entirely.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes a product tag in the format 'product:tax-001-mileage-log' (lowercase, hyphenated SKU slug — not 'TAX-001' or 'product:TAX-001')
expectations.evidence: Product tag is formatted as 'sku-tax-001' (generated by template literal stripping non-alphanumeric chars). The required 'product:tax-001-mileage-log' format is not used.

expectations.text: Answer mentions or references tag-dictionary.md as the source of truth for tag strings
expectations.evidence: No reference to tag-dictionary.md anywhere. Tag naming is invented from first principles without citing any project convention.

expectations.text: Answer references the tenant 'kebron' or the URL kebron.influencersoft.com somewhere (so the reader knows which tenant the call hits)
expectations.evidence: INFLUENCERSOFT_SUBDOMAIN hardcoded as 'kebron' with comment 'From CREDENTIALS.md: kebron.influencersoft.com'. Tenant clearly identified.

expectations.text: Answer does NOT log the full POST body or rpsKey (security: key leakage risk; the lib enforces this but the answer should respect it too)
expectations.evidence: Logs only 'Contact created/updated: ${email}' and 'Tags applied: ${allTags.join(...)}'. No POST body or rpsKey in any console.log call.

expectations.text: Mentions corporate-domain sender requirement (free domains like Gmail/Yahoo get DMARC-rejected)
expectations.evidence: Answer discusses SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration and alignment but never warns that free domains (Gmail, Yahoo) are DMARC-rejected by IS or that a corporate domain is required.

expectations.text: Mentions sender confirmation — that new sender emails are dead until the IS confirmation link is clicked
expectations.evidence: No mention anywhere of a sender confirmation link or the concept that new IS sender addresses must be activated before emails are live.

expectations.text: Mentions DKIM, SPF, AND DMARC DNS records (all three; not just 'DNS records')
expectations.evidence: Section 1 explicitly names all three: 'SPF record', 'DKIM signing', 'DMARC policy' — each with its own bullet and explanation.

expectations.evidence: Lists the 7 existing fields (sku_code, sku_label, bought_on, order_ref, xsell_name, xsell_url, pack_name) but frames them as positional references only — never mentions a naming convention or short-stem pattern.
